name: Balancing H2 + O2 → H2O
question_text: What are the correct coefficients when the equation H₂ + O₂ → H₂O is balanced using the smallest whole-number ratio?
general_feedback: The balanced equation is 2 H₂ + O₂ → 2 H₂O. Two molecules of H₂ provide 4 hydrogen atoms, one molecule of O₂ provides 2 oxygen atoms, and those react to form 2 molecules of H₂O (4 H, 2 O).
correct_feedback: Correct — Hydrogens: 4 on each side; oxygens: 2 on each side.

answers.feedback: Hydrogens: 4 on each side; oxygens: 2 on each side.
That leaves one extra oxygen atom on the left — the equation is not balanced.
Hydrogens do not balance: 2 on the left, 4 on the right.
Oxygens do not balance: 4 on the left, 2 on the right.
